worth_it_explanation: The search terms report shows the actual queries that triggered ads, and it is the main defense against spending on searches that will never convert. When no one reviews it and adds negatives, broad and phrase match keywords drift onto irrelevant queries and drain budget. The check is a read of the search terms report and the negative keyword setup. The fix is adding negatives, which takes effect immediately and reclaims spend without touching the rest of the account.
intro: The search terms report lists the real queries people typed before an ad showed, which is different from the keywords in the account. Broad and phrase match keywords let ads appear on related queries, so without regular review and negative keywords, a campaign can spend on searches that have nothing to do with the offer. These checks confirm that the search terms report is reviewed and that a negative keyword foundation keeps the matching from drifting onto irrelevant queries.

audit_checks.title: The search terms report is not reviewed, so irrelevant queries are never excluded
audit_checks.impact_rationale: The search terms report is where wasted spend shows up as queries that triggered an ad but do not fit the offer. When no one reviews it and adds negatives, that spend repeats every day, and the irrelevant traffic also feeds Smart Bidding a worse signal about who converts.

educate.base:
The search terms report shows the actual searches that caused an ad to show, which is not the same as the keyword list. A keyword on broad or phrase match can trigger an ad on many related queries, and some of those queries will not fit what the business sells. The report is where those mismatches become visible.

When the report is not reviewed, irrelevant queries keep triggering ads and the spend on them repeats. A plumber bidding on broad match might pay for searches about plumbing courses or plumbing jobs, neither of which is a customer. Beyond the wasted money, the clicks that do not convert feed Smart Bidding a noisier picture of who is worth bidding on, so the waste compounds.

The report lives under the campaign's Insights or Search terms view, and queries can be added as negative keywords from there. A few signs point to a report that is not being worked:
  • No negative keywords have been added in recent history despite broad or phrase match in use
  • The search terms report shows clearly irrelevant queries with spend against them
  • The same off-target query appears repeatedly across weeks
Reviewing the report on a regular cadence and adding negatives for the queries that do not fit reclaims the spend. You may want to add clearly irrelevant terms as negatives at the campaign or account level so one negative covers every campaign it applies to.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, broad match on product keywords often pulls in searches for brands the store does not carry or for free or used versions of a product. You may want to review the search terms report for competitor and 'free' queries and add them as negatives so the budget stays on sellable demand.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation account, service keywords often trigger on job-seeker and do-it-yourself queries that never become leads. You may want to add 'jobs', 'salary', and 'how to' style negatives so the spend stays on people looking to hire.

learn_more.source_title: Get negative keyword ideas using the search terms report

investigate.intro: Review the search terms report for irrelevant queries with spend and confirm whether negatives are being added.

google_ads_ui.label: Google Ads UI
steps: Open a campaign's Search terms report over a recent date range with meaningful spend.
Scan for queries that do not fit the offer and note whether they carry clicks or cost.
Check the negative keyword lists and recent change history to see whether negatives are being added. A report full of off-target queries with no recent negatives is the pattern to flag.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether the search terms report is reviewed and whether negatives are added for irrelevant queries. A report that is worked, with negatives added on a cadence, passes. A report showing off-target queries with spend and no recent negatives is a fail recorded with example queries and their cost.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether the search terms report is reviewed and whether negatives are being added to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s search terms report is not being reviewed, so irrelevant queries keep triggering ads with no negatives to stop them. The off-target clicks repeat as wasted spend and feed Smart Bidding a noisier signal about who converts. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the search terms report on a regular cadence and adding negatives for the queries that do not fit, using campaign or account-level negative lists so one entry covers every campaign it applies to.

audit_checks.title: Broad match runs without a maintained negative keyword foundation
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Broad match reaches the widest set of queries, so it depends on negatives to stay on target. Running it with few or no negatives lets spend spread to loosely related searches, and the wider the matching, the more the budget leaks before anyone reviews the report.

educate.base:
Broad match lets a keyword trigger ads on the widest range of related queries, including searches that share intent rather than words. Paired with Smart Bidding and a strong negative keyword foundation, it can find converting queries the account never thought to add. Without that negative foundation, the same width becomes a leak. The keyword reaches loosely related searches with no guardrail.

A negative keyword foundation is the set of standing exclusions that keep matching on target: competitor names the business does not want to pay for, job and career queries, free and do-it-yourself searches, and terms for products or services the business does not offer. When broad match runs without these, the account discovers the waste only after it has been spent, one search terms review at a time.

The keyword match types and negative lists show the setup. A few signs point to broad match without a foundation:
  • Keywords are on broad match while the account holds few or no negative keyword lists
  • The same kinds of irrelevant queries recur in the search terms report
  • Spend rose after a switch to broad match with no matching growth in negatives
Building and maintaining negative keyword lists before leaning on broad match keeps the width working for the account rather than against it. You may want to pair broad match with a conversion-based Smart Bidding strategy and a reusable negative list, so the matching has both a goal to optimize toward and a guardrail.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, broad match without negatives often spends on searches for competitors, marketplaces, and free versions of a product. You may want to build a standing negative list of competitor and marketplace names before widening match types.

investigate.intro: Confirm whether broad match keywords are backed by a maintained set of negative keywords.

google_ads_ui.label: Google Ads UI
steps: Review the keyword match types in the active campaigns and note where broad match is in use.
Open the negative keyword lists and the campaign-level negatives and gauge whether a real foundation exists.
Cross-check the search terms report for recurring irrelevant queries. Broad match with few negatives and recurring off-target queries is the pattern to flag.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether broad match keywords are backed by a maintained negative keyword foundation. Broad match paired with standing negatives and a conversion-based bid strategy passes. Broad match with few or no negatives, where the search terms report shows recurring waste, is a fail recorded with the gaps in the negative coverage.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether broad match keywords are backed by maintained negative keyword lists to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} runs broad match keywords without a maintained negative keyword foundation, so the widest match type has no guardrail. Spend spreads to loosely related queries, and the account discovers the waste only after it is spent. {{voice_recommend}} building reusable negative keyword lists for competitors, job and free queries, and unrelated products, then pairing broad match with a conversion-based bid strategy so the width works toward a goal rather than leaking.
